finding: Radiographic Findings in Total Hip Arthroplasty

differentials.name: Sequential bone/gallium scintigraphy (60-80% accurate, modest improvement over bone scintigraphy alone):
differentials.raw: Sequential bone/gallium scintigraphy (60-80% accurate, modest improvement over bone scintigraphy alone):

differentials.name: Combined labeled leukocyte-marrow scintigraphy = WBC/sulfur colloid scintigraphy (study of choice):
differentials.raw: Combined labeled leukocyte-marrow scintigraphy = WBC/sulfur colloid scintigraphy (study of choice):

NORMAL: irregular cement-bone interface =  normal interdigitation of PMMA (polymethylmethacrylate) with adjacent bone remodeling providing a mechanical interlock ◊  PMMA is not a glue!
Bone Scintigraphy (high NPV):: thin lucent line along cement-bone interface =  0.1-1.5-mm thin connective tissue membrane ("demarcation") along cement-bone interface accompanied by thin line of bone sclerosis

wide lucent zone at cement-bone interface =  ≥ 2-mm lucent line along bone-cement interface due to granulomatous membrane Cause:   component loosening ± reaction to particulate debris (eg, PMMA, polyethylene)

context: √  irregular cement-bone interface =  normal interdigitation of PMMA (polymethylmethacrylate) with adjacent bone remodeling providing a mechanical interlock ◊  PMMA is not a glue! √  thin lucent line along cement-bone interface =  0.1-1.5-mm thin connective tissue membrane ("demarcation") along cement-bone interface accompanied by thin line of bone sclerosis √  wide lucent zone at cement-bone interface =  ≥ 2-mm lucent line along bone-cement interface due to granulomatous membrane Cause:   component loosening ± reaction to particulate debris (eg, PMMA, polyethylene) √  lucent zone at metal-cem
